seoTitle: How to Print a Single Check From QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to print a single check in QuickBooks Online, from customizing the check format to selecting the right bank account before printing.
intro: Occasional check payments still come up even in mostly digital businesses, and QuickBooks Online has a streamlined way to handle them without extra hassle. This guide covers navigating to the check printing feature, customizing the check format, and verifying details before you print. It's aimed at small business owners and finance professionals who need to pay a supplier or reimburse an employee by check.

steps.heading: Navigate to the check printing feature
steps.body: Open QuickBooks Online and find the section for creating and printing checks.

steps.heading: Customize your check format
steps.body: Adjust the check layout before printing so it matches your check stock and business details.

steps.heading: Select the correct bank account
steps.body: Choose the bank account the check should be drawn from so your records stay accurate.

steps.heading: Verify check details for accuracy
steps.body: Double-check the payee, amount, and date before printing, and keep a copy of the check for your records.

faq.question: Can I print just one check at a time in QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: Yes, this tutorial covers producing a single check swiftly, whether for a supplier payment or an employee reimbursement.

faq.question: Do I need special check stock to print from QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: You'll need to customize your check format to match whatever check stock you're printing on before finalizing the print job.

faq.question: Why would a business still print checks instead of paying electronically?
faq.answer: Occasional check payments are still useful for contractors or vendors who prefer them, without the hassles of setting up electronic payment methods.

faq.question: Is this tutorial suitable for QuickBooks Online beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, it's beginner-friendly and also useful for seasoned users looking to refresh their skills.
